concepts.title: Absolute value functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Graphing the V-shaped function y = |x| and its transformations.

concepts.title: Arithmetic sequences
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sequences with a constant difference between terms, expressed with recursive or explicit formulas.

concepts.title: Combining like terms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Simplifying an expression by adding or subtracting terms that share the same variable and exponent.

concepts.title: Completing the square
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rewriting a quadratic as a perfect-square trinomial plus a constant to solve it or find the vertex.

concepts.title: The coordinate plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Plotting ordered pairs (x, y) on perpendicular axes to locate points in two dimensions.

concepts.title: The distributive property
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Multiplying a single term across a sum, a(b+c) = ab+ac, used to expand or factor expressions.

concepts.title: Domain and range
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The set of valid inputs (domain) and the set of resulting outputs (range) of a function.

concepts.title: Evaluating algebraic expressions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Substituting numeric values for variables in an expression and simplifying to a single value.

concepts.title: Properties of exponents
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rules for multiplying, dividing, and raising powers, including negative and zero exponents.

concepts.title: Exponential functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Functions of the form y = a·b^x whose graphs show rapid growth or decay.

concepts.title: Exponential growth and decay models
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling real situations like compound interest or population with exponential growth or decay factors.

concepts.title: Factoring quadratics
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rewriting a quadratic trinomial as a product of two binomials.

concepts.title: What is a function?
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A rule assigning exactly one output to each input, often shown as a mapping or with the vertical line test.

concepts.title: Function notation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using f(x) notation to name a function and evaluate its output for a given input.

concepts.title: Geometric sequences
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sequences with a constant ratio between terms, the discrete basis of exponential growth.

concepts.title: Graphing linear equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Drawing the straight-line graph of a linear equation using slope and intercepts or a table of points.

concepts.title: Graphing two-variable inequalities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Shading the half-plane of solutions to a linear inequality with a boundary line.

concepts.title: Interpreting function graphs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reading features such as intercepts, increasing/decreasing intervals, and maxima from a function's graph.

concepts.title: Rational vs. irrational numbers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Distinguishing numbers expressible as fractions from non-repeating, non-terminating decimals like √2 and π.

concepts.title: Solving linear inequalities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving inequalities like a single linear equation, flipping the inequality sign when multiplying or dividing by a negative.

concepts.title: Solving for a variable (literal equations)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rearranging a formula to isolate a chosen variable in terms of the others.

concepts.title: Multiplying binomials (FOIL)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Expanding the product of two binomials using the distributive property (FOIL) to form a quadratic.

concepts.title: One-step equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving an equation that requires a single inverse operation to isolate the variable.

concepts.title: Order of operations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The agreed sequence (parentheses, exponents, multiplication/division, addition/subtraction) for evaluating an arithmetic or algebraic expression.

concepts.title: Graphing parabolas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The U-shaped graph of a quadratic function, defined by its vertex, axis of symmetry, and direction of opening.

concepts.title: Parallel and perpendicular lines
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Recognizing that parallel lines share a slope and perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals.

concepts.title: Piecewise functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Functions defined by different rules over different intervals of the domain.

concepts.title: Point-slope form
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a line as y - y1 = m(x - x1) from a known point and slope.

concepts.title: The quadratic formula
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The formula x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac))/2a that solves any quadratic, with the discriminant indicating the number of real roots.

concepts.title: Radicals and square roots
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Simplifying square roots and other radicals, including products and quotients of radicals.

concepts.title: Rates and unit conversion
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using ratios and dimensional analysis to convert between units and reason about rates like speed.

concepts.title: Scientific notation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing very large or small numbers as a coefficient times a power of ten.

concepts.title: Slope of a line
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The steepness of a line measured as rise over run, the constant rate of change between any two points.

concepts.title: Slope-intercept form
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a line as y = mx + b, where m is the slope and b is the y-intercept.

concepts.title: Solving quadratics by factoring
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the zero-product property on a factored quadratic to find its roots.

concepts.title: Special products: difference of squares & perfect squares
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Recognizing patterns like a²-b² and (a±b)² to factor or expand quickly.

concepts.title: Standard form of a line
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a line as Ax + By = C and converting between standard and slope-intercept forms.

concepts.title: Solving systems by elimination
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Adding or subtracting equations to eliminate a variable and solve the system.

concepts.title: Solving systems by graphing
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the solution to a system of linear equations as the intersection point of their graphs.

concepts.title: Systems of inequalities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the overlapping shaded region that satisfies several linear inequalities at once.

concepts.title: Solving systems by substitution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving one equation for a variable and substituting that expression into the other equation.

concepts.title: Two-step equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving linear equations that require two inverse operations applied in the correct order.

concepts.title: Equations with variables on both sides
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving linear equations where the unknown appears on both sides by collecting variable terms together.

concepts.title: Vertex form of a quadratic
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a quadratic as y = a(x-h)² + k to read its vertex (h, k) directly and graph transformations.

concepts.title: x- and y-intercepts
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The points where a graph crosses the x-axis (y=0) and the y-axis (x=0).

concepts.title: Average rate of change
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The change in a function's output over an interval divided by the change in input, the slope of a secant line.

concepts.title: Comparing linear and exponential growth
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Contrasting constant additive growth with constant multiplicative growth and seeing why exponential eventually dominates.

concepts.title: Arithmetic with complex numbers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing complex numbers using conjugates.

concepts.title: The complex plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Representing complex numbers as points or vectors with real and imaginary axes, including modulus.

concepts.title: Composing functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Forming a new function f(g(x)) by feeding the output of one function into another.

concepts.title: Constructing models from data and constraints
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Translating verbal descriptions, tables, and constraints into equations, inequalities, or function models.

concepts.title: Direct and inverse variation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Relationships where one quantity is a constant multiple of another (direct) or of its reciprocal (inverse).

concepts.title: Polynomial division by quadratics
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using long division to divide a polynomial by a quadratic or higher-degree divisor.

concepts.title: Modulus and absolute value of complex numbers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The distance of a complex number from the origin in the complex plane, computed as the square root of the sum of squares.

concepts.title: End behavior of polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How the leading term's degree and sign determine the graph's behavior as x approaches infinity.

concepts.title: Evaluating logarithms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing logarithms by rewriting them as exponential equations, including common logs (base 10).

concepts.title: Even and odd functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Symmetry of functions about the y-axis (even) or origin (odd) and how to test for it.

concepts.title: Exponential growth and decay models
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building and interpreting exponential models, including continuous growth with the number e.

concepts.title: Factor and remainder theorems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Relating a polynomial's roots to its factors and using the remainder when dividing by (x-a).

concepts.title: Factoring higher-degree polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Factoring by grouping, factoring out common factors, and using sum/difference of cubes.

concepts.title: Transformations of functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Shifting, stretching, compressing, and reflecting graphs via changes to the function rule.

concepts.title: The fundamental theorem of algebra
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A degree-n polynomial has exactly n complex roots counting multiplicity, with non-real roots occurring in conjugate pairs.

concepts.title: Transforming absolute value and square root graphs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying shifts, stretches, and reflections to parent absolute-value and square-root functions.

concepts.title: Graphs of logarithmic functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The shape of y = log_b(x) as the reflection of an exponential graph across y = x, with its vertical asymptote.

concepts.title: Graphing polynomial functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sketching polynomial graphs from zeros, multiplicity, end behavior, and turning points.

concepts.title: Graphing radical functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The shape and domain restrictions of square-root and cube-root function graphs and their transformations.

concepts.title: Graphing sine and cosine
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sketching sinusoidal graphs and identifying amplitude, period, phase shift, and midline.

concepts.title: The imaginary unit i
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining i = √-1 and the cyclic powers of i.

concepts.title: Interpreting features of models in context
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Explaining what intercepts, maxima, asymptotes, and rates of change mean in a real-world model.

concepts.title: Inverse functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Functions that undo each other, found by swapping inputs and outputs and reflecting across y = x.

concepts.title: Introduction to logarithms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The logarithm as the inverse of exponentiation: log_b(x) answers 'b to what power gives x?'.

concepts.title: Logarithmic scales
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How logarithmic scales (pH, decibels, Richter) compress large ranges of values into manageable units.

concepts.title: Choosing a function model
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Selecting linear, quadratic, exponential, or other function families to fit and model real-world data.

concepts.title: Multiplying polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Distributing every term of one polynomial across another, generalizing FOIL to higher degrees.

concepts.title: The natural logarithm
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The logarithm base e (ln) and its role in continuous growth and solving equations involving e^x.

concepts.title: Adding and multiplying radical expressions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Combining like radicals and multiplying radical expressions, including binomials with radicals.

concepts.title: Modeling with piecewise functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building and graphing piecewise functions to model situations with different rules over different intervals.

concepts.title: Adding and subtracting polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Combining polynomials by adding or subtracting like terms across multiple variables and degrees.

concepts.title: Degree, terms, and standard form of polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Classifying polynomials by degree and number of terms and writing them in descending standard form.

concepts.title: Polynomial identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Verifying and using algebraic identities such as the difference of squares and Pascal's triangle for binomial expansion.

concepts.title: Polynomial long division
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Dividing one polynomial by another using a long-division algorithm.

concepts.title: Positive and negative intervals of polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Determining where a polynomial is above or below the x-axis using its zeros and a sign chart.

concepts.title: Properties of logarithms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The product, quotient, power, and change-of-base rules for logarithms.

concepts.title: The Pythagorean trig identity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Deriving sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 from the unit circle and using it to find one ratio from another.

concepts.title: Quadratics with complex roots
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the quadratic formula when the discriminant is negative to find complex conjugate roots.

concepts.title: Radian measure
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring angles as arc length per radius and converting between degrees and radians.

concepts.title: Rational exponents
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Interpreting fractional exponents as roots and applying exponent rules to them.

concepts.title: Rational functions and asymptotes
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Graphing ratios of polynomials, identifying vertical, horizontal, and slant asymptotes and holes.

concepts.title: Rational root theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Listing candidate rational roots of a polynomial as ratios of factors of the constant and leading coefficient.

concepts.title: Simplifying and rationalizing radicals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Simplifying radical expressions and rationalizing denominators.

concepts.title: Simplifying rational expressions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reducing, multiplying, dividing, adding, and subtracting fractions whose numerators and denominators are polynomials.

concepts.title: Solving exponential and logarithmic equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using logs and exponentials as inverse operations to solve equations with unknown exponents.

concepts.title: Completing the square in Algebra 2
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Completing the square to solve quadratics with complex solutions and to derive the quadratic formula.

concepts.title: Solving radical equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving equations containing radicals by isolating and raising to a power, checking for extraneous solutions.

concepts.title: Solving rational equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving equations with rational expressions by clearing denominators and checking for extraneous roots.

concepts.title: Systems of nonlinear equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding intersection points of a line with a circle or parabola, or two conics, by substitution.

concepts.title: Special polynomial products
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Patterns like (a+b)³ and sum/difference of cubes used to expand efficiently.

concepts.title: Synthetic division
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A shorthand procedure for dividing a polynomial by a linear factor (x-a).

concepts.title: Trig values of special angles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The exact sine, cosine, and tangent values at common angles like 30, 45, and 60 degrees from the unit circle.

concepts.title: The unit circle and radians
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Extending sine and cosine to all angles using coordinates on the unit circle, measured in radians.

concepts.title: Zeros and multiplicity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How the multiplicity of each root governs whether a graph crosses or touches the x-axis.

concepts.title: Absolute extrema and the candidates test
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the global maximum and minimum of a function on a closed interval by comparing values at critical points and endpoints. The extreme value theorem guarantees they exist.

concepts.title: Evaluating limits algebraically
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding limits using direct substitution, factoring, rationalizing, and limit laws. Handles indeterminate forms like 0/0 by algebraic simplification.

concepts.title: Antiderivatives and indefinite integrals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reversing differentiation to find a family of functions whose derivative is the integrand, written with a constant of integration. The building block of integral calculus.

concepts.title: Area between curves
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing the region enclosed by two functions by integrating their difference. The integrand is the top curve minus the bottom curve.

concepts.title: Average value and accumulation functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The average value of a function over an interval equals its integral divided by the interval length, and accumulation functions track running totals of a rate.

concepts.title: Average vs instantaneous rate of change
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Contrasting the slope of a secant over an interval with the slope of the tangent at a point, in applied contexts. The secant slope approaches the tangent slope as the interval shrinks.

concepts.title: The chain rule
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: composite, implicit, and inverse functions
concepts.description: The rule for differentiating composite functions: multiply the outer derivative by the inner derivative. The key to differentiating nested expressions.

concepts.title: Chain rule with trig, exponential, and log
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: composite, implicit, and inverse functions
concepts.description: Applying the chain rule to composites involving sine, e to a function, and natural log, producing derivatives like cos(g)·g′ and g′/g. The everyday workhorse of differentiation.

concepts.title: Concavity and inflection points
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The second derivative reveals whether a curve bends upward or downward, with inflection points where concavity switches. The second-derivative test classifies extrema.

concepts.title: Constant, sum, and difference rules
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
concepts.description: The basic linearity rules: the derivative of a constant is zero, and derivatives distribute across sums and differences with constant multiples factored out.

concepts.title: Continuity and the Intermediate Value Theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Classifying discontinuities and applying the IVT, which guarantees a continuous function hits every value between two endpoints. A core existence theorem.

concepts.title: Continuity over intervals and removing discontinuities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Establishing that a function is continuous across an interval and choosing parameter values that remove a discontinuity to make a piecewise function continuous.

concepts.title: Curve sketching
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Assembling intercepts, asymptotes, intervals of increase and decrease, concavity, and inflection points into a full graph of a function. Synthesizes the derivative tests.

concepts.title: The definite integral
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The exact signed area under a curve, defined as the limit of Riemann sums. It accumulates the total change of a quantity over an interval.

concepts.title: The derivative as a function
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
concepts.description: Viewing the derivative itself as a new function giving the slope at every input, and sketching f′ directly from the graph of f. Connects local slope to a global curve.

concepts.title: The derivative as a limit
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
concepts.description: Defining the derivative as the limit of the difference quotient, the slope of the secant line approaching the tangent line. The formal foundation of differentiation.

concepts.title: Derivatives of inverse functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: composite, implicit, and inverse functions
concepts.description: Finding the slope of an inverse function as the reciprocal of the original function's slope at the corresponding point, including inverse trig derivatives.

concepts.title: Derivatives of trig, exponential, and log functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
concepts.description: The standard derivatives of sin, cos, e^x, and ln(x), which recur throughout calculus. e^x is notable for being its own derivative.

concepts.title: Differentiability and continuity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
concepts.description: Why differentiability implies continuity but not the reverse, with corners, cusps, and vertical tangents as places a continuous function fails to be differentiable.

concepts.title: Exponential growth and decay
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling situations where a quantity's rate of change is proportional to its size, giving exponential solutions. Describes populations, radioactive decay, and interest.

concepts.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, Part 1
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The derivative of an accumulation function equals the integrand evaluated at the upper limit. Shows that integration and differentiation undo each other.

concepts.title: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The theorem linking differentiation and integration: integration is the inverse of differentiation, and a definite integral equals the change in an antiderivative.

concepts.title: Higher-order derivatives
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
concepts.description: Differentiating repeatedly to get the second, third, and further derivatives, where the second derivative measures how the rate of change is itself changing.

concepts.title: Implicit differentiation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: composite, implicit, and inverse functions
concepts.description: Differentiating equations not solved for y by treating y as a function of x and applying the chain rule. Needed for curves like circles and ellipses.

concepts.title: Critical points and extrema
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the first derivative to find where a function rises or falls and to locate local maxima and minima at critical points. The first-derivative test classifies them.

concepts.title: Infinite limits and vertical asymptotes
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: When a function grows without bound near a point, the limit is infinite and the graph has a vertical asymptote. Determined by a denominator approaching zero.

concepts.title: L'Hopital's rule
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Resolving indeterminate limits of the form 0/0 or infinity/infinity by taking the ratio of derivatives. A powerful tool linking limits and differentiation.

concepts.title: Estimating limits from graphs and tables
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reading the value a function approaches by inspecting its graph or a table of nearby inputs, including one-sided behavior. Builds intuition before algebraic methods.

concepts.title: Limit laws
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The algebraic rules letting limits of sums, products, quotients, and powers be computed from the limits of the parts. The bookkeeping behind evaluating limits.

concepts.title: Limits at infinity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The end behavior of a function as the input grows without bound, which reveals horizontal asymptotes. Determined by comparing growth rates of terms.

concepts.title: Linear approximation and differentials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Approximating a function near a point using its tangent line, since a smooth curve looks linear up close. Differentials estimate small changes in output.

concepts.title: The Mean Value Theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: On a differentiable interval, there is some point where the instantaneous rate of change equals the average rate of change. Geometrically, a tangent parallel to the secant.

concepts.title: Motion: position, velocity, and acceleration
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Velocity is the derivative of position and acceleration is the derivative of velocity, linking calculus to physical motion along a line. Sign of velocity indicates direction.

concepts.title: Motion problems with integrals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Recovering velocity from acceleration and displacement from velocity by integrating, and distinguishing total distance from net displacement using absolute value.

concepts.title: One-sided limits
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Limits taken approaching a point strictly from the left or right, whose agreement is required for the two-sided limit to exist. Central to piecewise functions.

concepts.title: Optimization
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the maximum or minimum of a real-world quantity by modeling it as a function and locating its critical points. Used to minimize cost or maximize area.

concepts.title: The power rule
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
concepts.description: A shortcut for differentiating powers of x: bring the exponent down and decrease it by one. The most-used basic derivative rule.

concepts.title: Product and quotient rules
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: definition and basic derivative rules
concepts.description: Rules for differentiating products and quotients of functions, since the derivative of a product is not the product of derivatives. Essential algebraic differentiation tools.

concepts.title: Properties of definite integrals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rules for combining integrals: reversing limits flips the sign, splitting intervals adds, and constants factor out. Manipulate integrals without recomputing.

concepts.title: Related rates
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using a known rate of change to find an unknown related rate, by differentiating a relationship with respect to time. Classic problems involve filling tanks or sliding ladders.

concepts.title: Setting up related rates problems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The modeling workflow of choosing variables, writing a geometric relationship, differentiating with respect to time, and substituting known rates last. The strategy behind related rates.

concepts.title: Riemann sums as a limit and integral notation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Refining a Riemann sum by letting rectangle width shrink to zero, passing to the integral as the exact limit. Explains where the integral sign and dx come from.

concepts.title: Riemann sums
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Approximating the area under a curve by summing rectangles of fixed width, using left, right, or midpoint heights. The limit of these sums defines the definite integral.

concepts.title: The second derivative test
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Classifying a critical point as a local max or min by the sign of the second derivative there, where concave up gives a minimum and concave down a maximum.

concepts.title: Separation of variables
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving a differential equation by collecting each variable on its own side and integrating both. The main analytic method for first-order equations in AB.

concepts.title: Slope fields
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A grid of short tangent segments showing the slope a differential equation prescribes at each point. Visualizes the family of solution curves without solving.

concepts.title: The squeeze theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: If a function is trapped between two others that share a common limit, it must share that limit too. Used for tricky limits like sin(x)/x.

concepts.title: Tangent lines to implicit curves
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Differentiation: composite, implicit, and inverse functions
concepts.description: Using implicit differentiation to find the slope and equation of a tangent line to a curve like a circle or ellipse at a given point. Handles curves that fail the vertical line test.

concepts.title: Types of discontinuities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Classifying breaks in a graph as removable holes, jump discontinuities, or infinite (asymptotic) discontinuities. Each arises from a distinct limit behavior.

concepts.title: Integration by u-substitution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reversing the chain rule for integration by substituting a new variable to simplify the integrand. The primary technique of antidifferentiation in AB.

concepts.title: Verifying solutions to differential equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Confirming a proposed function solves a differential equation by substituting it and its derivatives back in. Distinguishes general families from particular solutions.

concepts.title: Volumes by cylindrical shells
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the volume of a solid of revolution by summing thin cylindrical shells parallel to the axis of rotation. An alternative to disks when slicing perpendicular is awkward.

concepts.title: Volumes with known cross sections
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing the volume of a solid whose perpendicular cross-sections are known shapes (squares, triangles, semicircles) by integrating their areas along the axis.

concepts.title: Volumes of revolution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the volume of a solid formed by rotating a region about an axis, using the disk or washer method. Each cross-section is a circular slice.

concepts.title: Absolute vs conditional convergence
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Distinguishing series that converge even after taking absolute values from those that converge only because of cancellation. Absolute convergence is the stronger property.

concepts.title: Alternating series test and error
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A series whose terms alternate sign and shrink monotonically to zero converges, and its truncation error is bounded by the first omitted term.

concepts.title: Arc length
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The length of a curve, found by integrating the infinitesimal hypotenuse of horizontal and vertical changes along it. Generalizes the distance formula to curves.

concepts.title: Area between polar curves
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Computing the region between two polar curves by integrating one-half the difference of the squared radii over the relevant angle interval. Requires finding intersection angles.

concepts.title: Derivatives and arc length of parametric curves
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Finding dy/dx and arc length for parametric curves using derivatives with respect to the parameter. Slope is (dy/dt) divided by (dx/dt).

concepts.title: Area and slope of polar curves
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Computing the area enclosed by a polar curve with the (1/2) integral of r-squared, and finding slopes by converting to parametric form. Calculus in the polar setting.

concepts.title: Direct and limit comparison tests
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Deciding convergence by comparing a series term-by-term, or by a limiting ratio, with a known benchmark such as a p-series or geometric series.

concepts.title: Convergence of improper integrals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Deciding whether an improper integral converges or diverges, including p-integrals and comparison with simpler integrands. Mirrors series convergence reasoning.

concepts.title: Euler's method
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Approximating a solution to a differential equation by stepping forward along tangent line segments. A numerical method that follows a slope field.

concepts.title: Euler's method step size and error
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Examining how a smaller step size improves the accuracy of Euler's method and whether the approximation over- or under-estimates based on concavity.

concepts.title: Improper integrals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Integrals with infinite bounds or unbounded integrands, evaluated as limits. They may converge to a finite value or diverge.

concepts.title: Integral test and p-series
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Tying a series' convergence to that of a corresponding improper integral, and classifying p-series as convergent exactly when p exceeds one. Includes the harmonic series.

concepts.title: Integration by parts
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reversing the product rule for integration, using the formula integral of u dv equals uv minus integral of v du. Handles products like x times e^x.

concepts.title: Logistic growth models
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A differential equation where growth slows as a population nears a carrying capacity, producing an S-shaped curve. More realistic than pure exponential growth.

concepts.title: Maclaurin series of common functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The memorized series for eˣ, sin x, cos x, and 1/(1 − x), and building new series by substituting, differentiating, or integrating these. A toolkit for representing functions.

concepts.title: Speed and distance for vector motion
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Finding speed as the magnitude of the velocity vector and total distance traveled by integrating speed over time for a particle moving in the plane.

concepts.title: The nth-term test for divergence
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: If a series' terms do not approach zero, the series must diverge. A quick first screen that can never prove convergence.

concepts.title: Differentiating and integrating power series
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building new series by differentiating or integrating a known power series term by term within its interval of convergence. Generates series for related functions.

concepts.title: Parametric equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Describing a curve by expressing x and y as separate functions of a parameter t, capturing direction and timing of motion. Traces paths a single equation cannot.

concepts.title: Second derivatives of parametric curves
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Computing the second derivative of y with respect to x for a parametric curve by differentiating dy/dx again with respect to t and dividing by dx/dt. Reveals parametric concavity.

concepts.title: Integration with partial fractions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Decomposing a rational function into a sum of simpler fractions that can be integrated individually. Extends the integration toolkit to many quotients.

concepts.title: Polar coordinates
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Locating points by distance r from the origin and angle theta, an alternative to Cartesian coordinates. Natural for circular and spiral curves.

concepts.title: Polar and Cartesian conversion
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Translating equations and points between polar (r, θ) and Cartesian (x, y) using x = r cos θ and y = r sin θ. Lets polar curves be analyzed in either system.

concepts.title: Power series and interval of convergence
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A series in powers of (x - a) that represents a function on an interval where it converges. The radius and interval of convergence bound where it is valid.

concepts.title: The ratio test
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the limiting ratio of consecutive terms to test convergence, with a limit under one giving convergence. The primary tool for series with factorials or exponentials.

concepts.title: Sequences and convergence
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: An ordered list of numbers and whether its terms approach a limit. The foundation for studying infinite series.

concepts.title: Convergence tests for series
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Tools such as the nth-term, integral, comparison, ratio, and alternating series tests to decide whether an infinite series sums to a finite value.

concepts.title: Solving the logistic differential equation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Analyzing the logistic model to find the carrying capacity, the fastest-growth point at half capacity, and the long-run limit of the population. Extracts behavior without fully solving.

concepts.title: Taylor and Maclaurin series
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Representing a function as an infinite polynomial built from its derivatives at a point, with truncated versions approximating the function nearby.

concepts.title: Taylor polynomial error bounds
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Estimating how far a truncated Taylor polynomial deviates from the true function using the Lagrange error bound or alternating series remainder.

concepts.title: Trigonometric substitution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Integrating expressions with square roots of quadratics by substituting a trig function, using a right-triangle picture to convert back. Handles forms like the square root of a² − x².

concepts.title: Vector-valued functions and motion
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Parametric equations, polar coordinates, and vector-valued functions
concepts.description: Functions returning a position vector, whose derivatives give velocity and acceleration vectors. Models motion of a particle in the plane.

concepts.title: Bar charts and segmented bar charts
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Displaying counts or proportions of a categorical variable with bar charts, and comparing groups with side-by-side or segmented bars.

concepts.title: Binomial distribution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling the number of successes in a fixed number of independent trials, and computing binomial probabilities, mean, and standard deviation.

concepts.title: Boxplots and the five-number summary
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Exploring one-variable quantitative data: Summary statistics
concepts.description: Summarizing a distribution with the five-number summary and comparing groups with parallel boxplots and the 1.5*IQR outlier rule.

concepts.title: Central limit theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Explaining why the sampling distribution of the mean becomes approximately normal as sample size grows, regardless of population shape.

concepts.title: Chi-square tests for categorical data
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Testing goodness-of-fit, independence, and homogeneity by comparing observed and expected counts with the chi-square statistic.

concepts.title: Combining and transforming random variables
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the mean and variance of sums, differences, and linear transformations of random variables.

concepts.title: Conditional probability and independence
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Updating probabilities given information, testing for independence, and using tree diagrams for multi-stage events.

concepts.title: Confidence interval for a mean (t-interval)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the t-distribution to build a confidence interval for a population mean when the population standard deviation is unknown.

concepts.title: Confidence interval for a proportion
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Constructing and interpreting a confidence interval for a population proportion using the standard error and a critical z-value.

concepts.title: Discrete random variables: mean and variance
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Describing a discrete distribution with a probability table and computing expected value and variance.

concepts.title: Dot plots, histograms, and stem-and-leaf plots
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Exploring one-variable quantitative data: Displaying and describing
concepts.description: Visualizing the distribution of one quantitative variable and describing its shape, center, and spread.

concepts.title: Experiments, control, and randomization
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Designing experiments with treatments, control, randomization, replication, and blocking to support causal conclusions.

concepts.title: Geometric distribution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling the number of trials until the first success, and computing geometric probabilities and expected value.

concepts.title: Hypothesis testing: null, alternative, and p-values
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Framing null and alternative hypotheses, computing a test statistic, and interpreting the p-value against a significance level.

concepts.title: Inference for regression slopes
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Constructing confidence intervals and significance tests for the slope of a least-squares regression line.

concepts.title: Least-squares regression line
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Fitting a line that minimizes squared residuals and interpreting its slope and intercept in context.

concepts.title: Measures of center and spread
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Exploring one-variable quantitative data: Summary statistics
concepts.description: Computing and interpreting mean, median, range, IQR, variance, and standard deviation as summaries of a distribution.

concepts.title: The normal distribution and the empirical rule
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling bell-shaped data with the normal curve and using the 68-95-99.7 rule to estimate proportions.

concepts.title: Normal probability and percentile calculations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding areas under the normal curve to compute probabilities and inverse-normal values to find cutoffs from percentiles.

concepts.title: Permutations and combinations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Counting ordered arrangements and unordered selections to compute probabilities of complex events.

concepts.title: Basic probability rules and Venn diagrams
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing probabilities using complement, addition, and multiplication rules, visualized with Venn diagrams.

concepts.title: Residuals, residual plots, and R-squared
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using residuals to assess fit, reading residual plots for non-linear patterns, and interpreting the coefficient of determination.

concepts.title: Sampling distributions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Understanding how a statistic varies from sample to sample, including the sampling distribution of the sample mean and proportion.

concepts.title: Sampling methods and bias
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Comparing simple random, stratified, cluster, and systematic sampling, and recognizing sources of sampling bias.

concepts.title: Scatterplots and correlation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Displaying the relationship between two quantitative variables and quantifying linear strength and direction with the correlation coefficient r.

concepts.title: Describing distributions: shape, skew, and outliers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Exploring one-variable quantitative data: Displaying and describing
concepts.description: Characterizing a distribution as symmetric or skewed, unimodal or multimodal, and identifying gaps and outliers.

concepts.title: Two-sample tests for means and proportions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Comparing two groups with two-sample t-tests and two-proportion z-tests, including paired designs.

concepts.title: Two-way tables and marginal/conditional distributions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Organizing two categorical variables in a contingency table and reading off marginal, joint, and conditional distributions to detect association.

concepts.title: Type I and Type II errors and power
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Distinguishing false-positive and false-negative errors and understanding how sample size and effect size affect the power of a test.

concepts.title: Z-scores and standardization
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Converting values to z-scores to express position in standard-deviation units and comparing values across different distributions.

concepts.title: Autonomous equations, equilibria, and stability
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Analyzing equations whose rate depends only on the state, locating equilibrium solutions and classifying them as stable or unstable with a phase line.

concepts.title: Complex roots and oscillatory solutions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Interpreting complex characteristic roots as sinusoidal solutions, linking the algebra to oscillating physical systems.

concepts.title: Convolution and the Laplace transform
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the convolution theorem to invert a product of transforms and to express the response of a system to an arbitrary input.

concepts.title: Euler's method
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Numerically approximating a solution by stepping along the slope field in small increments.

concepts.title: Exact equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Recognizing an equation as the total differential of a potential function and solving it by reconstructing that function.

concepts.title: Exponential growth and decay models
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling quantities whose rate of change is proportional to their size, including population growth, decay, and Newton's law of cooling.

concepts.title: First-order linear equations and integrating factors
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving linear first-order equations by multiplying through by an integrating factor to make the left side an exact derivative.

concepts.title: Introduction to differential equations and slope fields
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Interpreting a differential equation as a rule for slopes and visualizing all solution curves with a direction (slope) field.

concepts.title: Inverse Laplace transform and partial fractions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Recovering a time-domain function from its transform, using partial-fraction decomposition to match table entries.

concepts.title: Laplace transforms of common functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building the transform table for exponentials, sines, cosines, and polynomials, and applying shift and derivative properties.

concepts.title: Solving initial value problems with the Laplace transform
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Transforming a differential equation and its initial conditions into an algebraic equation, solving, and inverting to obtain the solution.

concepts.title: The Laplace transform: definition and properties
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining the Laplace transform as an integral operator that maps functions of time to functions of a complex frequency variable, and establishing its linearity.

concepts.title: The logistic growth model
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling growth that saturates at a carrying capacity, producing the characteristic S-shaped solution curve.

concepts.title: Modeling with first-order equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Translating real situations such as mixing tanks, falling bodies with drag, and cooling into first-order differential equations and solving them.

concepts.title: Repeated characteristic roots
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Handling the degenerate case where the characteristic equation has a double root, introducing the extra t times exponential solution.

concepts.title: Forced oscillations and resonance
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Studying how a periodically forced oscillator responds, with amplitude growing dramatically when the forcing frequency matches the natural frequency.

concepts.title: RLC circuits as second-order systems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling charge and current in a resistor-inductor-capacitor circuit with a second-order linear equation analogous to the spring-mass system.

concepts.title: Second-order linear homogeneous equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving constant-coefficient homogeneous equations via the characteristic equation, with real, repeated, or complex roots.

concepts.title: Separable equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving first-order equations by separating variables onto opposite sides and integrating each independently.

concepts.title: Spring-mass systems, damping, and resonance
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling a mass on a spring as a second-order equation and classifying underdamped, critically damped, overdamped, and resonant behavior.

concepts.title: Step functions, impulses, and the Dirac delta
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the unit step (Heaviside) function and the Dirac delta to model switches and instantaneous forcing, and transforming them with the Laplace transform.

concepts.title: Systems of differential equations and the phase plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Representing coupled first-order equations as a vector field in the phase plane and reading trajectories, nodes, spirals, and saddles.

concepts.title: Nonhomogeneous equations and undetermined coefficients
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding a particular solution to a forced equation by guessing a form matching the forcing term and combining it with the homogeneous solution.

concepts.title: Variation of parameters
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding a particular solution to any forced linear equation by varying the constants of the homogeneous solution, using the Wronskian.

concepts.title: Angle relationships
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Complementary, supplementary, vertical, and adjacent angle relationships, and angles formed by parallel lines and a transversal.

concepts.title: Arc length and sector area
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing arc length and sector area as fractions of a circle's circumference and area, including radian intuition.

concepts.title: Area of triangles and polygons
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Formulas for the area of triangles, parallelograms, trapezoids, and regular polygons.

concepts.title: Area and perimeter on the coordinate plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the perimeter and area of polygons whose vertices are given as coordinates.

concepts.title: Bisectors and triangle centers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Perpendicular bisectors, angle bisectors, medians, and altitudes and the centers (circumcenter, incenter, centroid) they form.

concepts.title: Cavalieri's principle
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Two solids with equal cross-sectional areas at every height have equal volume, justifying oblique-solid volume formulas.

concepts.title: Arcs, central and inscribed angles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Relationships between arc measures, central angles, and inscribed angles in a circle.

concepts.title: Composing transformations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying a sequence of transformations and finding the single transformation equivalent to the composition.

concepts.title: Congruent triangle criteria
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, and HL conditions that guarantee two triangles are congruent.

concepts.title: Cross-sections and solids of revolution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The 2D shapes formed by slicing a solid and the 3D solids generated by rotating a 2D region.

concepts.title: Inscribed shapes and cyclic quadrilaterals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Properties of polygons inscribed in a circle, including that opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary.

concepts.title: Density and 3D modeling
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying volume and surface-area formulas to real objects, including density (mass per unit volume) problems.

concepts.title: Dilations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Scaling a figure larger or smaller from a center point by a scale factor, changing size but not shape.

concepts.title: Distance and midpoint formulas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing the distance between and midpoint of two points using coordinates.

concepts.title: Dividing line segments
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding a point that partitions a segment in a given ratio using coordinates.

concepts.title: Ellipses and hyperbolas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Standard-form equations and key features (foci, axes) of ellipses and hyperbolas.

concepts.title: Equation of a circle
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The standard form (x-h)² + (y-k)² = r² describing a circle by center and radius.

concepts.title: Parallel & perpendicular lines on the coordinate plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using slope criteria to write equations of lines parallel or perpendicular to a given line through a point.

concepts.title: Geometric constructions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building figures such as perpendicular bisectors, angle bisectors, and equilateral triangles using only a compass and straightedge.

concepts.title: Triangle similarity in right triangles (geometric mean)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The altitude to the hypotenuse creates similar sub-triangles, giving geometric-mean relationships among the segments.

concepts.title: Line and angle proofs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing logical proofs about angles and parallel lines using definitions, postulates, and the transitive property.

concepts.title: Parabolas as conic sections
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining a parabola by its focus and directrix and deriving its equation.

concepts.title: Proofs with parallelograms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using coordinates and slope/distance to prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram, rectangle, or other special figure.

concepts.title: Polygon angle sums
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The interior angles of an n-gon sum to (n-2)·180 degrees, and exterior angles sum to 360 degrees.

concepts.title: Properties of quadrilaterals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining properties of parallelograms, rectangles, rhombi, squares, and trapezoids.

concepts.title: Proportionality in triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Theorems such as the triangle proportionality (side-splitter) theorem arising from parallel lines cutting triangles.

concepts.title: The Pythagorean theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: In a right triangle, a² + b² = c², relating the legs to the hypotenuse.

concepts.title: Reflections
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Flipping a figure across a line of reflection to produce a mirror image.

concepts.title: Right triangle trig ratios (SOH-CAH-TOA)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining sine, cosine, and tangent as ratios of sides in a right triangle.

concepts.title: Rigid transformations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Translations, rotations, and reflections that move a figure without changing its size or shape.

concepts.title: Rotations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Turning a figure about a fixed center point by a given angle.

concepts.title: Scale factor and area/volume ratios
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: When a figure is scaled by factor k, its area scales by k² and its volume by k³.

concepts.title: Similar triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Triangles with equal angles and proportional sides, established by AA, SAS, or SSS similarity.

concepts.title: Solving right triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using trig ratios and inverse trig to find unknown sides and angles in right triangles, including angles of elevation and depression.

concepts.title: Solving problems with similar figures
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using proportions from similar figures to find missing lengths, including indirect measurement and scale drawings.

concepts.title: Special right triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The fixed side ratios of 45-45-90 and 30-60-90 triangles.

concepts.title: Surface area of solids
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing the total surface area of prisms, cylinders, cones, pyramids, and spheres from their nets.

concepts.title: Symmetry
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Line (reflective) and rotational symmetry of figures, where a transformation maps the figure onto itself.

concepts.title: Tangents, chords, and secants
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Properties of tangent lines, chords, and secants of a circle and the angle and length relationships among them.

concepts.title: Translations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sliding every point of a figure the same distance and direction in the plane.

concepts.title: Triangle angle sum
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The interior angles of any triangle sum to 180 degrees, with an exterior-angle corollary.

concepts.title: Triangle congruence proofs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Two-column or paragraph proofs establishing that triangles are congruent and using CPCTC.

concepts.title: Triangle inequality theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The sum of any two side lengths of a triangle must exceed the third side, constraining which triangles can exist.

concepts.title: Volume of prisms and cylinders
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing volume as base area times height for prisms and cylinders.

concepts.title: Volume of pyramids, cones, and spheres
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Volume formulas for pyramids, cones (one-third base times height), and spheres (4/3 π r³).

concepts.title: Basis of a subspace
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Choosing a minimal set of independent vectors that spans a subspace, giving every vector unique coordinates.

concepts.title: Change of basis
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Expressing the same vector or transformation in a different coordinate system using a change-of-basis matrix.

concepts.title: Column space, null space, and rank
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Describing the image (column space) and kernel (null space) of a transformation and relating their dimensions through the rank-nullity theorem.

concepts.title: Composition of transformations and matrix multiplication
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Understanding matrix multiplication as applying one transformation after another.

concepts.title: Cross product
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Producing a vector perpendicular to two given vectors in 3D, with magnitude equal to the area of the parallelogram they span.

concepts.title: The determinant
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Interpreting the determinant as the factor by which a transformation scales area or volume, with sign indicating orientation.

concepts.title: The determinant as area and volume
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Seeing the absolute determinant as the area of the parallelogram or volume of the parallelepiped spanned by a matrix's columns.

concepts.title: Eigenbasis and diagonalization
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using an eigenbasis to write a transformation as a diagonal matrix, making powers and dynamics easy to compute.

concepts.title: Dimension and rank
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Counting the number of independent directions in a space or the column space of a matrix to capture its true size.

concepts.title: Dot product and orthogonality
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring length, angle, and projection between vectors, and recognizing orthogonality when the dot product is zero.

concepts.title: Eigenbasis
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Assembling a basis entirely of eigenvectors so a transformation acts as simple independent stretches along each axis.

concepts.title: Eigenvalues and eigenvectors
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding directions a transformation only stretches or compresses (eigenvectors) and the corresponding scale factors (eigenvalues).

concepts.title: Functions and transformations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Treating a transformation as a function between vector spaces, with domain, codomain, image, and preimage.

concepts.title: Solving linear systems with Gaussian elimination
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Row-reducing an augmented matrix to reduced row echelon form to solve a system of linear equations.

concepts.title: Orthonormal bases and Gram-Schmidt
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Constructing an orthonormal basis from any basis via the Gram-Schmidt process, simplifying projections and coordinates.

concepts.title: Image and preimage of a transformation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Tracking where a set of points lands under a transformation (image) and which inputs map to a given output (preimage).

concepts.title: Linear combinations and span
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building new vectors as weighted sums of others and describing the span as the set of all reachable vectors (a line, plane, or all of space).

concepts.title: Linear independence
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Determining whether a set of vectors contains redundancy, i.e. whether any vector lies in the span of the others.

concepts.title: Finding the matrix of a linear transformation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building the matrix of a linear transformation by recording where it sends each standard basis vector as a column.

concepts.title: Inverse transformations and matrix inverses
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Undoing a transformation with its inverse matrix, which exists precisely when the determinant is nonzero.

concepts.title: Matrix multiplication: rows, columns, and mechanics
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing matrix products entry by entry as row-by-column dot products and understanding the dimension-matching rule.

concepts.title: Matrices as linear transformations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Interpreting matrix-vector multiplication as a linear transformation that moves space, sending basis vectors to the matrix columns.

concepts.title: Orthogonal complements
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Splitting a space into a subspace and the set of all vectors orthogonal to it, the perpendicular companion used in projections.

concepts.title: Orthogonal projections and least squares
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Projecting a vector onto a subspace to find the closest approximation, the geometric basis of least-squares fitting.

concepts.title: Orthonormal bases and coordinates
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using a basis of mutually perpendicular unit vectors so that coordinates come directly from dot products and projections are simple.

concepts.title: Rotation, reflection, and projection matrices
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reading off the standard 2D and 3D matrices that rotate, reflect across a line or plane, or project onto a subspace.

concepts.title: Span as the set of reachable vectors
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Visualizing the span of vectors as everything you can reach by scaling and adding them: a line, a plane, or all of space.

concepts.title: Subspaces of R-n
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identifying subsets closed under addition and scalar multiplication, such as lines and planes through the origin, that behave like smaller vector spaces.

concepts.title: Subspaces, basis, and dimension
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining a subspace and choosing a minimal spanning, independent set (a basis) whose size is the dimension.

concepts.title: The transpose of a matrix
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Flipping a matrix across its diagonal, swapping rows and columns, and its role in dot products, symmetry, and least squares.

concepts.title: Magnitude, unit vectors, and normalization
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring a vector's length and scaling it to a unit vector that keeps direction but has magnitude one.

concepts.title: Vectors and vector operations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Representing vectors as arrows or coordinate lists and combining them with addition and scalar multiplication.

concepts.title: Change of variables and the Jacobian determinant
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Transforming an integral to new coordinates, with the Jacobian determinant accounting for how the substitution stretches area or volume.

concepts.title: Curl of a vector field
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring the local spinning of a vector field, with the curl vector pointing along the axis of rotation by the right-hand rule.

concepts.title: Cylindrical and spherical coordinate systems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Locating points in space by radius and angles, the natural coordinates for cylinders, cones, and spheres.

concepts.title: Directional derivatives
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring the rate of change of a surface as you step in any chosen unit direction, computed as the gradient dotted with that direction.

concepts.title: Divergence of a vector field
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring how much a vector field acts as a source or sink at each point, the net outward flow per unit area or volume.

concepts.title: Divergence and curl
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Local measures of how a vector field spreads out (divergence) and rotates (curl) at a point.

concepts.title: Divergence theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Green's, Stokes', and the divergence theorems
concepts.description: Relating the flux of a vector field through a closed surface to the triple integral of its divergence over the enclosed solid.

concepts.title: Double integrals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Integrating a function over a planar region to compute the volume under a surface, evaluated as an iterated integral.

concepts.title: Double integrals in polar coordinates
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Evaluating double integrals over circular and annular regions by switching to polar coordinates with the r dr dθ area element.

concepts.title: Flux of a 3D vector field through a surface
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring the net flow of a three-dimensional vector field across an oriented surface using the outward unit normal.

concepts.title: Functions of several variables
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Extending the idea of a function to several inputs and outputs, including scalar fields, parametric curves, and vector-valued functions.

concepts.title: Conservative fields and the fundamental theorem of line integrals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Recognizing gradient (conservative) fields whose line integrals depend only on endpoints, evaluated via a potential function.

concepts.title: The gradient and directional derivatives
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Packaging partial derivatives into the gradient vector, which points in the direction of steepest ascent and is perpendicular to level curves.

concepts.title: Gradient descent intuition
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Following the negative gradient downhill on a surface to iteratively approach a minimum, the geometric idea behind optimization algorithms.

concepts.title: Graphs, level curves, and contour maps
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Visualizing a function of two variables as a surface in 3D and as contour lines (level curves) in the plane.

concepts.title: Green's theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Green's, Stokes', and the divergence theorems
concepts.description: Relating a line integral around a closed planar curve to a double integral of curl over the region it encloses.

concepts.title: The Jacobian matrix and local linearization
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the matrix of partial derivatives to linearly approximate a transformation near a point and measure local area/volume scaling.

concepts.title: Constrained optimization and Lagrange multipliers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Optimizing a function subject to a constraint by aligning the gradients of the objective and constraint surfaces.

concepts.title: The Laplacian
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The divergence of the gradient, measuring how a point's value compares to the average of its neighbors, central to heat flow and harmonic functions.

concepts.title: Line integrals in scalar and vector fields
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Integrating along a curve to compute arc-length-weighted quantities or the work done by a vector field along a path.

concepts.title: The multivariable chain rule
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Differentiating compositions where inputs depend on other variables, tracking contributions through a tree of dependencies.

concepts.title: Applied optimization with multivariable functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using critical points and boundary analysis to maximize or minimize quantities like volume, cost, or distance in applied settings.

concepts.title: Optimizing multivariable functions: critical points and the second-derivative test
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding maxima, minima, and saddle points where the gradient vanishes and classifying them with the second-partial-derivative test.

concepts.title: Parametric curves and surfaces
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Describing curves and surfaces in space by mapping parameters to position vectors, the foundation for line and surface integrals.

concepts.title: Partial derivatives
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring the rate of change of a multivariable function with respect to one variable while holding the others fixed, seen as a slope of a slice of the surface.

concepts.title: Integration in polar, cylindrical, and spherical coordinates
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Changing coordinates to simplify integrals over circular and spherical regions, with the Jacobian supplying the area/volume element.

concepts.title: Quadratic approximation and the Hessian
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Approximating a surface with a second-order paraboloid using second partial derivatives collected in the Hessian matrix.

concepts.title: Second partial derivatives and Clairaut's theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Taking derivatives twice to measure concavity and twist of a surface, with mixed partials equal under continuity (Clairaut's theorem).

concepts.title: Stokes' theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.unit: Green's, Stokes', and the divergence theorems
concepts.description: Generalizing Green's theorem to surfaces in space, relating the circulation around a boundary curve to the curl flux through the surface.

concepts.title: Surface area of parametrized surfaces
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing the area of a curved surface by integrating the magnitude of the cross product of its tangent vectors over the parameter domain.

concepts.title: Surface integrals and flux
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Integrating over a parametrized surface to compute area or the flux of a vector field through the surface.

concepts.title: Tangent planes and local linear approximation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Approximating a surface near a point with the tangent plane defined by partial derivatives.

concepts.title: Three-dimensional graphs of two-variable functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reading and building the surface z = f(x, y) above the xy-plane, including peaks, valleys, ridges, and saddles.

concepts.title: Transformations and mappings of space
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Viewing a vector-valued function as a map that warps one region of space into another, bending grids and distorting areas.

concepts.title: Triple integrals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Integrating over a three-dimensional solid region to compute volume, mass, or average value.

concepts.title: 2D curl and 2D divergence
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The scalar curl and divergence of a planar vector field, capturing rotation and expansion of flow in the plane.

concepts.title: Vector fields
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Assigning a vector to every point in the plane or space, visualized as a field of arrows representing flow, force, or velocity.

concepts.title: Vector-valued functions and space curves
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Tracing a moving point through space as a parameter advances, with derivative and integral giving velocity and displacement along the curve.

concepts.title: Visualizing scalar-valued functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Comparing ways to picture a scalar field of several variables: graphs, contour maps, and color-coded heat maps of its values.

concepts.title: Work done by a force field along a path
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing the work a vector force field does on a particle moving along a curve as the line integral of the field dotted with displacement.

concepts.title: Arithmetic series
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A sum of terms with a constant difference between consecutive terms, with a closed form equal to the number of terms times the average of first and last.

concepts.title: The binomial theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Expanding (a + b)ⁿ using binomial coefficients from Pascal's triangle, linking combinatorics to algebra. Each coefficient counts a selection.

concepts.title: Equations of circles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The set of points a fixed distance from a center, with standard equation (x − h)² + (y − k)² = r². The simplest conic, obtained by completing the square.

concepts.title: Arithmetic with complex numbers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing complex numbers, including using the conjugate to rationalize denominators. Multiplication scales and rotates in the plane.

concepts.title: The complex plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A 2D plane where a complex number a + bi is plotted with real part on the horizontal axis and imaginary part on the vertical axis. Distance from origin gives the modulus.

concepts.title: Composing functions
concepts.subject: math
