concepts.description: Building a new function by feeding the output of one function into another, written (f∘g)(x) = f(g(x)). The domain of the composite is constrained by both functions.

concepts.title: Conic sections from a cone
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How circles, ellipses, parabolas, and hyperbolas all arise as cross-sections of a double cone at different cutting angles. Unifies the four conic curves geometrically.

concepts.title: Continuity of functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A function is continuous at a point when its limit there equals its value there, producing a graph with no breaks, jumps, or holes.

concepts.title: Converting between rectangular and polar form
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Translating a complex number between a + bi and r(cos θ + i sin θ) using the modulus and argument. Each form makes different operations easier.

concepts.title: De Moivre's theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Raising a complex number in polar form to a power by raising the modulus and multiplying the angle. Also yields the n distinct nth roots of a complex number.

concepts.title: Determinant and matrix inverse
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The determinant measures how a matrix scales area, and a nonzero determinant means the matrix is invertible. The inverse undoes the transformation.

concepts.title: Domain of composite functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Determining the valid inputs of (f∘g)(x) by intersecting the domain of g with the inputs whose g-outputs lie in the domain of f. Restrictions can come from either function.

concepts.title: The dot product
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A scalar formed from two vectors that encodes the angle between them, zero exactly when they are perpendicular. Used for projection and work.

concepts.title: Eccentricity of conic sections
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A single number measuring how stretched a conic is, distinguishing circles, ellipses, parabolas, and hyperbolas. Unifies the conics on a continuous scale.

concepts.title: Ellipses
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The set of points whose distances to two foci sum to a constant. Defined by its center, major axis, and minor axis.

concepts.title: Expected value
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The long-run average outcome of a random process, computed by weighting each value by its probability. Used to evaluate games and decisions.

concepts.title: Geometric series
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A sum of terms with a constant ratio between consecutive terms. Finite geometric series have a closed-form sum; infinite ones converge when the ratio is between -1 and 1.

concepts.title: Finite geometric series formula
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Deriving and applying the closed-form sum a(1 − rⁿ)/(1 − r) for a finite geometric series, avoiding term-by-term addition. Foundational for annuities and decay.

concepts.title: Graphs of inverse trig functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Plotting arcsin, arccos, and arctan as reflections of the restricted trig graphs across y = x, with their characteristic bounded ranges and horizontal asymptotes.

concepts.title: Graphing sinusoidal functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Plotting transformations of sine and cosine, controlled by amplitude, period, phase shift, and midline. These parameters model periodic real-world phenomena.

concepts.title: Hyperbolas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The set of points whose distances to two foci differ by a constant, forming two opening branches with asymptotes. A conic from slicing both nappes of a cone.

concepts.title: The identity matrix
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The square matrix with ones on the diagonal that leaves any vector or matrix unchanged under multiplication. The multiplicative identity for matrices.

concepts.title: Infinite geometric series
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Summing infinitely many geometric terms, which converges to a/(1 − r) when the common ratio has absolute value less than one. Otherwise the sum diverges.

concepts.title: Introduction to limits
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The value a function approaches as the input nears a point, independent of the value at that point. The conceptual gateway to calculus.

concepts.title: Inverse functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A function that undoes another, reflecting its graph across the line y = x. Inverses exist only when the original function is one-to-one.

concepts.title: Inverse trigonometric functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Functions like arcsin, arccos, and arctan that recover an angle from a trig ratio, defined on restricted domains to remain one-to-one.

concepts.title: Law of sines and law of cosines
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Relations that solve non-right triangles from given sides and angles. The law of cosines generalizes the Pythagorean theorem to any angle.

concepts.title: Matrices as transformations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Interpreting a 2x2 matrix as a linear transformation of the plane that rotates, scales, reflects, or shears space. The columns show where the basis vectors land.

concepts.title: Determinant as signed area
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Interpreting the determinant of a 2x2 matrix as the signed area of the parallelogram spanned by its columns, with sign showing orientation. Zero means the columns are parallel.

concepts.title: Matrix operations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Adding, scaling, and multiplying matrices, where multiplication combines rows and columns. Matrix multiplication is not commutative.

concepts.title: One-sided limits and limit existence
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Examining the value a function approaches from only the left or only the right, where the two-sided limit exists only when both agree. Reveals jumps and breaks.

concepts.title: Parabolas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The set of points equidistant from a focus and a directrix, forming a U-shaped curve. A conic section formed by slicing a cone parallel to its side.

concepts.title: Permutations and combinations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Counting arrangements where order matters (permutations) versus selections where it does not (combinations). The building blocks of combinatorial probability.

concepts.title: Polar form of complex numbers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Representing a complex number by its modulus and argument, r(cos θ + i sin θ). This form makes multiplication and powers geometrically intuitive.

concepts.title: Probability of compound events
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Combining probabilities with the addition rule for unions and the multiplication rule for independent events, accounting for overlap and conditioning.

concepts.title: Probability using combinatorics
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing probabilities of compound events by counting favorable and total outcomes. Combines counting rules with the ratio definition of probability.

concepts.title: Radians and arc length
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring angles by the arc length they subtend on a unit circle, where a full turn is 2π radians. Arc length equals radius times the angle in radians.

concepts.title: Sigma notation and finite sums
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Compact notation for sums using the Greek letter sigma with an index, lower bound, and upper bound. Used to express arithmetic and geometric series.

concepts.title: Solving systems with matrices
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a linear system as a matrix equation Ax = b and solving it with the inverse matrix or row reduction. Connects matrices to systems of equations.

concepts.title: Solving trigonometric equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding all angles that satisfy a trig equation, using the unit circle and periodicity to capture infinitely many solutions. Inverse trig gives the principal solution.

concepts.title: Special angle trig values
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The exact sine, cosine, and tangent values at 30, 45, and 60 degrees and their multiples, derived from special right triangles and placed around the unit circle.

concepts.title: Tangent and reciprocal trig functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining tangent as sine over cosine, plus the reciprocal functions secant, cosecant, and cotangent. Their graphs feature vertical asymptotes where the denominator is zero.

concepts.title: Transformations of functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Shifting, stretching, and reflecting a graph by modifying its input and output, the algebraic source of composite-function structure. Inside changes affect x, outside changes affect y.

concepts.title: Trigonometric identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Equations true for all angles, including the Pythagorean, angle-sum, and double-angle identities. They are used to simplify expressions and solve trig equations.

concepts.title: The unit circle
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A circle of radius 1 centered at the origin used to define sine and cosine as the coordinates of a point at a given angle. It links angle, arc length, and the trig functions.

concepts.title: Vector applications: force and velocity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling real situations like net force, navigation, and relative velocity by resolving vectors into components and summing them. Connects vectors to physics.

concepts.title: Magnitude and direction of vectors
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing a vector's length with the Pythagorean theorem and its direction angle with inverse tangent, then recovering components from magnitude and angle.

concepts.title: Vector operations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Adding vectors tip-to-tail, scaling them, and finding the resultant. Scalar multiplication stretches or reverses direction.

concepts.title: Vectors and their components
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Quantities with both magnitude and direction, expressed by components or as magnitude-and-angle. The basis for modeling forces, velocities, and displacements.

concepts.title: Verifying inverse functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Confirming two functions are inverses by checking that both compositions f(g(x)) and g(f(x)) return the input x. The algebraic test for invertibility.

concepts.title: The ambiguous case (SSA)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: When the law of sines is given SSA, there may be zero, one, or two possible triangles.

concepts.title: Amplitude, period, and phase shift
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How coefficients in y = a·sin(b(x-c))+d stretch, compress, and shift a sinusoidal graph.

concepts.title: Angle sum and difference identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identities for sin(A±B), cos(A±B), and tan(A±B) used to rewrite combined angles.

concepts.title: Area of a triangle using trig
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing triangle area as (1/2)ab·sin C from two sides and the included angle.

concepts.title: Bearings and navigation problems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying the laws of sines and cosines to real-world navigation, surveying, and bearing problems.

concepts.title: Cofunction and complementary angle identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The relationship sin θ = cos(90° - θ) and other cofunction identities arising from complementary angles in a right triangle.

concepts.title: Double-angle and half-angle identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identities for sin(2θ), cos(2θ), and half-angle expressions derived from the sum formulas.

concepts.title: Graphs of tangent and reciprocal functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The periodic graphs of tangent, cotangent, secant, and cosecant and their asymptotes.

concepts.title: Graphs of sine and cosine
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Tracing the unit circle into the wave-shaped graphs of sine and cosine.

concepts.title: Inverse trigonometric functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining arcsine, arccosine, and arctangent as functions with restricted domains so they pass the vertical line test.

concepts.title: Inverse trig in right triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using arcsine, arccosine, and arctangent to find unknown angles from side ratios.

concepts.title: Law of cosines
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The generalization c² = a² + b² - 2ab·cos C used to solve triangles given SAS or SSS.

concepts.title: Law of sines
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The relationship a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C used to solve non-right triangles.

concepts.title: Midline and vertical translation of trig graphs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How the constant d in a sinusoid raises or lowers the midline about which the wave oscillates.

concepts.title: Modeling with right triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying trig to angle-of-elevation, depression, and other real-world right-triangle problems.

concepts.title: Modeling with sinusoidal functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Fitting sine or cosine functions to periodic phenomena like tides, daylight hours, or oscillation.

concepts.title: Product-to-sum and sum-to-product formulas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identities that convert products of sines and cosines into sums and vice versa, useful for simplifying expressions.

concepts.title: Proving trigonometric identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Transforming one side of a proposed identity using known identities until it matches the other side.

concepts.title: The Pythagorean identity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The identity sin²θ + cos²θ = 1, derived from the unit circle.

concepts.title: Pythagorean theorem review
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reviewing a² + b² = c² as the foundation for right-triangle trigonometry.

concepts.title: Radians and degree conversion
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring angles as arc length on the unit circle and converting between radians and degrees.

concepts.title: Reciprocal and quotient identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identities expressing tangent as sine over cosine and the reciprocal relationships among the six trig functions.

concepts.title: Reciprocal trig ratios
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Cosecant, secant, and cotangent as reciprocals of sine, cosine, and tangent.

concepts.title: Reference angles and signs by quadrant
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the acute reference angle and the quadrant to determine the value and sign of a trig function for any angle.

concepts.title: Right triangle word problems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Setting up and solving multi-step real-world problems by drawing and labeling right triangles.

concepts.title: Sine, cosine, and tangent ratios
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining the three primary trig ratios from the sides of a right triangle (SOH-CAH-TOA).

concepts.title: Solving trigonometric equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding all angle solutions to equations involving trig functions, using the unit circle and periodicity.

concepts.title: Exact values at special angles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The exact sine, cosine, and tangent values at 30°, 45°, 60°, and their multiples around the unit circle.

concepts.title: Finding all ratios from one trig value
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Given one trig ratio, reconstructing the right triangle to find the remaining five trig ratios.

concepts.title: The unit circle
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining sine and cosine as the y- and x-coordinates of a point on the unit circle for any angle.
